title: Incursa.Quic Pending Outbound Stream Open Work Item
summary: Implement pending outbound stream opens that remain queued when the peer limit is exhausted and resume only after later real `MAX_STREAMS` growth makes the requested direction available.
planned_changes:
- Track pending outbound open requests by request ID and requested direction so blocked opens can stay queued instead of failing fast.
- Retry queued opens only when a real `MAX_STREAMS` frame raises the matching peer limit.
- Preserve cancellation and terminal-state completion semantics so a queued open cannot be revived after it has been settled.
- Add focused requirement-home proof for pending behavior, later real `MAX_STREAMS` wake-up, and cancellation while blocked.
verification_plan: Run the new `REQ-QUIC-API-0014` requirement-home proof plus the existing `REQ-QUIC-API-0008` and `REQ-QUIC-API-0009` regression coverage, then run `git diff --check` and the repo-local SpecTrace render/check flow for the changed canonical JSON artifacts.
completion_notes: This slice keeps the blocked open request honest by staying pending until the peer really grows the matching stream limit. It does not add `STREAMS_BLOCKED` emission or broader stream-management parity.
